22	// closing the Slowloris hole that a bare http.Server leaves open (bodyclose /
23	// gosec hygiene). The endpoint is loopback-only, but the bound is cheap.
24	const readHeaderTimeout = 5 * time.Second
25	
26	// ErrNonLoopbackBind is returned when the requested listen address is not
27	// loopback. The metrics endpoint exposes per-path operational data (endpoints,
28	// loss, liveness) and must never be reachable off-host by default; binding it to
29	// a routable or wildcard address is refused at construction (fail fast).
30	var ErrNonLoopbackBind = errors.New("metrics: listen address must be loopback (127.0.0.0/8 or ::1)")
31	
32	// Server is the localhost-bound Prometheus /metrics endpoint. It owns a private
33	// registry fed by a Source; it never touches the global default registry.
34	type Server struct {
35		ln  net.Listener
36		srv *http.Server
37		log log.Logger
38	}
39	
40	// NewServer validates that addr is loopback, binds a TCP listener, and wires the
41	// /metrics handler over a private registry fed by src. It returns
42	// ErrNonLoopbackBind WITHOUT binding if addr is not loopback. addr is a
43	// host:port string; a ":0" or "127.0.0.1:0" port yields an OS-assigned port
44	// readable via Addr after construction.
45	func NewServer(addr string, src Source, logger log.Logger) (*Server, error) {
46		if err := requireLoopback(addr); err != nil {
47			return nil, err
48		}
49	
50		reg := prometheus.NewRegistry()
51		if err := reg.Register(NewCollector(src)); err != nil {
52			return nil, fmt.Errorf("metrics: register collector: %w", err)
53		}
54	
55		ln, err := net.Listen("tcp", addr)
56		if err != nil {
57			return nil, fmt.Errorf("metrics: listen on %q: %w", addr, err)
58		}
59		// Act-then-verify: net.Listen performs its OWN address resolution,
60		// independent of the requireLoopback pre-check above. For a hostname listen
61		// address a resolver whose answers differ between the two lookups (TOCTOU)
62		// could get the endpoint bound to a routable interface even though the
63		// pre-check passed. Assert the address the kernel actually bound is
64		// loopback and fail closed otherwise — this removes any dependence on
65		// resolver trust.
66		if err := verifyLoopbackBind(ln.Addr()); err != nil {
67			_ = ln.Close()
68			return nil, err
69		}
70	
71		mux := http.NewServeMux()
72		mux.Handle(metricsPath, promhttp.HandlerFor(reg, promhttp.HandlerOpts{
73			ErrorHandling: promhttp.HTTPErrorOnError,
74		}))
75	
76		return &Server{
77			ln: ln,
78			srv: &http.Server{
79				Handler:           mux,
80				ReadHeaderTimeout: readHeaderTimeout,
81			},
82			log: logger.Component("metrics"),
83		}, nil
84	}
85	
86	// Addr returns the actual bound listen address (with the resolved port).
87	func (s *Server) Addr() net.Addr { return s.ln.Addr() }
88	
89	// URL returns the scrape URL for the endpoint, suitable for the Fetch helper.
90	func (s *Server) URL() string {
91		return fmt.Sprintf("http://%s%s", s.ln.Addr().String(), metricsPath)
92	}
93	
94	// Start serves the endpoint in a background goroutine and returns immediately.
95	// A Serve error other than the expected http.ErrServerClosed (from Close) is
96	// logged; the listener is already bound, so Start itself cannot fail.
97	func (s *Server) Start() {
98		s.log.Info("metrics endpoint listening", "addr", s.ln.Addr().String(), "path", metricsPath)
99		go func() {
100			if err := s.srv.Serve(s.ln); err != nil && !errors.Is(err, http.ErrServerClosed) {
101				s.log.Error("metrics endpoint serve error", "err", err.Error())
102			}
103		}()
104	}
105	
106	// Close gracefully shuts the endpoint down, bounded by ctx.
107	func (s *Server) Close(ctx context.Context) error {
108		return s.srv.Shutdown(ctx)
109	}
110	
111	// requireLoopback enforces that addr's host is a loopback address. An IP literal
112	// must satisfy IsLoopback; a hostname (e.g. "localhost") is accepted only if it
113	// resolves to at least one address and EVERY resolved address is loopback — a
114	// hostname that maps to any routable address is refused. An empty host (":9095")
115	// binds every interface and is refused.
116	func requireLoopback(addr string) error {
117		host, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(addr)
118		if err != nil {
119			return fmt.Errorf("metrics: invalid listen address %q: %w", addr, err)
120		}
121		if host == "" {
122			return fmt.Errorf("%w: empty host in %q binds all interfaces", ErrNonLoopbackBind, addr)
123		}
124	
125		if ip, err := netip.ParseAddr(host); err == nil {
126			if !ip.IsLoopback() {
127				return fmt.Errorf("%w: got %q", ErrNonLoopbackBind, addr)
128			}
129			return nil
130		}
131	
132		// Hostname: resolve and require every resolved address to be loopback.
133		ips, err := net.LookupIP(host)
134		if err != nil {
135			return fmt.Errorf("metrics: cannot resolve listen host %q: %w", host, err)
136		}
137		if len(ips) == 0 {
138			return fmt.Errorf("metrics: listen host %q resolved to no addresses", host)
139		}
140		for _, ip := range ips {
141			if !ip.IsLoopback() {
142				return fmt.Errorf("%w: host %q resolves to non-loopback %s", ErrNonLoopbackBind, host, ip)
143			}
144		}
145		return nil
146	}
147	
148	// verifyLoopbackBind is the fail-closed, act-then-verify half of the loopback
149	// guard: it asserts that the address the kernel ACTUALLY bound (ln.Addr()) is a
150	// loopback TCP address, independent of any DNS resolution. Because net.Listen
151	// resolves a hostname listen address itself — a second, independent lookup from
152	// requireLoopback's — verifying the concrete bound address is the only check
153	// that a resolver cannot subvert. A non-TCP or non-loopback bound address
154	// yields ErrNonLoopbackBind so the caller can close the listener and refuse.
155	func verifyLoopbackBind(a net.Addr) error {
156		tcp, ok := a.(*net.TCPAddr)
157		if !ok {
158			return fmt.Errorf("%w: bound to non-TCP address %v", ErrNonLoopbackBind, a)
159		}
160		if !tcp.IP.IsLoopback() {
161			return fmt.Errorf("%w: bound to non-loopback %s", ErrNonLoopbackBind, tcp.IP)
162		}
163		return nil
164	}

410	// shards (M learned from a parity shard) with at least one data frame missing; at
411	// that point every still-missing data index is reconstructed and returned once,
412	// and the group is marked complete. Offering to a completed group returns nil.
413	func (d *Decoder) Offer(s Shard) ([]Recovered, error) {
414		g := s.GroupID()
415		if !d.admitGroup(g) {
416			// SUSPECT wire GroupID (a lone junk/forged id, or a not-yet-corroborated
417			// reopen): drop the shard without touching the frontier so one forged frame
418			// cannot evict live groups or blackhole subsequent real ones.
419			return nil, nil
420		}
421	
422		gs := d.state(g)
423		if gs.done {
424			return nil, nil
425		}
426	
427		switch sh := s.(type) {
428		case DataShard:
429			// A data index can never be valid at or above maxShards-K: RS admits at most
430			// maxShards total shards, so any group has m <= maxShards-K data shards and
431			// valid indices 0..m-1. Rejecting an out-of-range index here — before it is
432			// buffered — bounds a single group's data index space (a flood of distinct
433			// bogus indices can no longer grow gs.data without bound) and stops one bogus
434			// shard from being buffered to permanently poison the group.
435			if sh.Index < 0 || sh.Index >= maxShards-d.cfg.ParityShards {
436				return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: data shard index %d out of range [0,%d) in group %d", sh.Index, maxShards-d.cfg.ParityShards, sh.Group)
437			}
438			buf := make([]byte, len(sh.Payload))
439			copy(buf, sh.Payload)
440			gs.data[sh.Index] = buf
441		case ParityShard:
442			if err := gs.observeParity(sh, d.cfg.ParityShards); err != nil {
443				return nil, err
444			}
445		default:
446			// Unreachable: Shard is a closed sum over DataShard and ParityShard.
447			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: unknown shard type %T", s)
448		}
449	
450		return d.maybeReconstruct(g, gs)
451	}
452	
453	// observeParity records a parity shard and pins/checks the group's cardinality
454	// and shard geometry, failing fast on an inconsistent group.
455	func (gs *groupState) observeParity(p ParityShard, parityShards int) error {
456		// A parity position is only ever 0..K-1. Rejecting an out-of-range index here —
457		// before it is buffered — bounds gs.parity to at most K entries and keeps a bogus
458		// index out of the group's state entirely.
459		if p.Index < 0 || p.Index >= parityShards {
460			return fmt.Errorf("fec: parity index %d out of range [0,%d) for group %d", p.Index, parityShards, p.Group)
461		}
462		if p.DataCount < 1 {
463			return fmt.Errorf("fec: parity for group %d carries invalid DataCount %d", p.Group, p.DataCount)
464		}
465		// DataCount is the group cardinality m carried on the wire. RS admits m+K <=
466		// maxShards, so m can never exceed maxShards-K. Reject an oversized DataCount
467		// here, before maybeReconstruct runs its O(m) missing-index scan and allocates
468		// an m+K shard table: a bogus DataCount near 2^31 would otherwise force a
469		// multi-billion-iteration loop and a multi-gigabyte allocation before
470		// reedsolomon.New rejected it.
471		if maxData := maxShards - parityShards; p.DataCount > maxData {
472			return fmt.Errorf("fec: parity for group %d carries DataCount %d exceeding max %d", p.Group, p.DataCount, maxData)
473		}
474		// A parity payload IS one RS shard, so its length is the group's uniform shard
475		// length. Reject a payload too short to hold a data shard's length prefix:
476		// trusting it would pin shardLen < lenPrefixLen and later panic encodeDataShard
477		// (fec.go). The library must not trust wire-derived geometry.
478		if len(p.Payload) < lenPrefixLen {
479			return fmt.Errorf("fec: parity for group %d has payload %d bytes, shorter than the %d-byte length prefix", p.Group, len(p.Payload), lenPrefixLen)
480		}
481		if gs.dataCount == -1 {
482			gs.dataCount = p.DataCount
483		} else if gs.dataCount != p.DataCount {
484			return fmt.Errorf("fec: inconsistent DataCount for group %d: %d vs %d", p.Group, gs.dataCount, p.DataCount)
485		}
486		if gs.shardLen == -1 {
487			gs.shardLen = len(p.Payload)
488		} else if gs.shardLen != len(p.Payload) {
489			return fmt.Errorf("fec: inconsistent shard length for group %d: %d vs %d", p.Group, gs.shardLen, len(p.Payload))
490		}
491		buf := make([]byte, len(p.Payload))
492		copy(buf, p.Payload)
493		gs.parity[p.Index] = buf
494		return nil
495	}
496	
497	// maybeReconstruct attempts to complete a group once its cardinality is known.
498	func (d *Decoder) maybeReconstruct(g GroupID, gs *groupState) ([]Recovered, error) {
499		if gs.dataCount == -1 {
500			return nil, nil // M not yet known; keep buffering
501		}
502		m := gs.dataCount
503	
504		// A buffered data shard whose index is within the static wire bound (accepted at
505		// Offer time, when m was not yet known) but >= this group's actual cardinality m
506		// cannot belong to the group. Drop it rather than wedging the group: leaving it
507		// buffered would make every reconstruct attempt fail on the stale out-of-range
508		// index, so a later valid <=K shard set could never recover. Dropping it also
509		// releases its buffer.
510		for idx := range gs.data {
511			if idx >= m {
512				delete(gs.data, idx)
513			}
514		}
515	
516		missing := make([]int, 0)
517		for i := 0; i < m; i++ {
518			if _, ok := gs.data[i]; !ok {
519				missing = append(missing, i)
520			}
521		}
522		if len(missing) == 0 {
523			gs.markDone() // all data present; nothing to recover
524			return nil, nil
525		}
526	
527		if len(gs.data)+len(gs.parity) < m {
528			return nil, nil // fewer than M shards survive; recovery impossible so far
529		}
530	
531		codec, err := d.codec(m)
532		if err != nil {
533			return nil, err
534		}
535	
536		shards := make([][]byte, m+d.cfg.ParityShards)
537		for idx, payload := range gs.data {
538			// encodeDataShard's precondition is shardLen >= lenPrefixLen+len(payload).
539			// A wire-derived data payload that overruns the group's shard length would
540			// otherwise be silently truncated while its length prefix records the full
541			// length, feeding RS inconsistent bytes and fabricating recovered payloads.
542			// Fail fast instead of trusting the geometry.
543			if lenPrefixLen+len(payload) > gs.shardLen {
544				return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: data shard %d payload (%d bytes) does not fit group %d shard length %d", idx, len(payload), g, gs.shardLen)
545			}
546			shards[idx] = encodeDataShard(payload, gs.shardLen)
547		}
548		for j, pb := range gs.parity {
549			if j < 0 || j >= d.cfg.ParityShards {
550				return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: parity index %d out of range for group %d (K=%d)", j, g, d.cfg.ParityShards)
551			}
552			shards[m+j] = pb
553		}
554	
555		if err := codec.Reconstruct(shards); err != nil {
556			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: reed-solomon reconstruct group %d (m=%d k=%d): %w", g, m, d.cfg.ParityShards, err)
557		}
558	
559		sort.Ints(missing)
560		recovered := make([]Recovered, 0, len(missing))
561		for _, idx := range missing {
562			payload, err := decodeDataShard(shards[idx])
563			if err != nil {
564				return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: decode reconstructed shard %d of group %d: %w", idx, g, err)
565			}
566			recovered = append(recovered, Recovered{Group: g, Index: idx, Payload: payload})
567		}
568		d.recovered += uint64(len(recovered))
569		gs.markDone()
570		return recovered, nil
571	}

574	func (d *Decoder) codec(m int) (reedsolomon.Encoder, error) {

410	// shards (M learned from a parity shard) with at least one data frame missing; at
411	// that point every still-missing data index is reconstructed and returned once,
412	// and the group is marked complete. Offering to a completed group returns nil.
413	func (d *Decoder) Offer(s Shard) ([]Recovered, error) {
414		g := s.GroupID()
415		if !d.admitGroup(g) {
416			// SUSPECT wire GroupID (a lone junk/forged id, or a not-yet-corroborated
417			// reopen): drop the shard without touching the frontier so one forged frame
418			// cannot evict live groups or blackhole subsequent real ones.
419			return nil, nil
420		}
421	
422		gs := d.state(g)
423		if gs.done {
424			return nil, nil
425		}
426	
427		switch sh := s.(type) {
428		case DataShard:
429			// A data index can never be valid at or above maxShards-K: RS admits at most
430			// maxShards total shards, so any group has m <= maxShards-K data shards and
431			// valid indices 0..m-1. Rejecting an out-of-range index here — before it is
432			// buffered — bounds a single group's data index space (a flood of distinct
433			// bogus indices can no longer grow gs.data without bound) and stops one bogus
434			// shard from being buffered to permanently poison the group.
435			if sh.Index < 0 || sh.Index >= maxShards-d.cfg.ParityShards {
436				return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: data shard index %d out of range [0,%d) in group %d", sh.Index, maxShards-d.cfg.ParityShards, sh.Group)
437			}
438			buf := make([]byte, len(sh.Payload))
439			copy(buf, sh.Payload)
440			gs.data[sh.Index] = buf
441		case ParityShard:
442			if err := gs.observeParity(sh, d.cfg.ParityShards); err != nil {
443				return nil, err
444			}
445		default:
446			// Unreachable: Shard is a closed sum over DataShard and ParityShard.
447			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: unknown shard type %T", s)
448		}
449	
450		return d.maybeReconstruct(g, gs)
451	}
452	
453	// observeParity records a parity shard and pins/checks the group's cardinality
454	// and shard geometry, failing fast on an inconsistent group.
455	func (gs *groupState) observeParity(p ParityShard, parityShards int) error {
456		// A parity position is only ever 0..K-1. Rejecting an out-of-range index here —
457		// before it is buffered — bounds gs.parity to at most K entries and keeps a bogus
458		// index out of the group's state entirely.
459		if p.Index < 0 || p.Index >= parityShards {
460			return fmt.Errorf("fec: parity index %d out of range [0,%d) for group %d", p.Index, parityShards, p.Group)
461		}
462		if p.DataCount < 1 {
463			return fmt.Errorf("fec: parity for group %d carries invalid DataCount %d", p.Group, p.DataCount)
464		}
465		// DataCount is the group cardinality m carried on the wire. RS admits m+K <=
466		// maxShards, so m can never exceed maxShards-K. Reject an oversized DataCount
467		// here, before maybeReconstruct runs its O(m) missing-index scan and allocates
468		// an m+K shard table: a bogus DataCount near 2^31 would otherwise force a
469		// multi-billion-iteration loop and a multi-gigabyte allocation before
470		// reedsolomon.New rejected it.
471		if maxData := maxShards - parityShards; p.DataCount > maxData {
472			return fmt.Errorf("fec: parity for group %d carries DataCount %d exceeding max %d", p.Group, p.DataCount, maxData)
473		}
474		// A parity payload IS one RS shard, so its length is the group's uniform shard
475		// length. Reject a payload too short to hold a data shard's length prefix:
476		// trusting it would pin shardLen < lenPrefixLen and later panic encodeDataShard
477		// (fec.go). The library must not trust wire-derived geometry.
478		if len(p.Payload) < lenPrefixLen {
479			return fmt.Errorf("fec: parity for group %d has payload %d bytes, shorter than the %d-byte length prefix", p.Group, len(p.Payload), lenPrefixLen)
480		}
481		if gs.dataCount == -1 {
482			gs.dataCount = p.DataCount
483		} else if gs.dataCount != p.DataCount {
484			return fmt.Errorf("fec: inconsistent DataCount for group %d: %d vs %d", p.Group, gs.dataCount, p.DataCount)
485		}
486		if gs.shardLen == -1 {
487			gs.shardLen = len(p.Payload)
488		} else if gs.shardLen != len(p.Payload) {
489			return fmt.Errorf("fec: inconsistent shard length for group %d: %d vs %d", p.Group, gs.shardLen, len(p.Payload))
490		}
491		buf := make([]byte, len(p.Payload))
492		copy(buf, p.Payload)
493		gs.parity[p.Index] = buf
494		return nil
495	}
496	
497	// maybeReconstruct attempts to complete a group once its cardinality is known.
498	func (d *Decoder) maybeReconstruct(g GroupID, gs *groupState) ([]Recovered, error) {
499		if gs.dataCount == -1 {
500			return nil, nil // M not yet known; keep buffering
501		}
502		m := gs.dataCount
503	
504		// A buffered data shard whose index is within the static wire bound (accepted at
505		// Offer time, when m was not yet known) but >= this group's actual cardinality m
506		// cannot belong to the group. Drop it rather than wedging the group: leaving it
507		// buffered would make every reconstruct attempt fail on the stale out-of-range
508		// index, so a later valid <=K shard set could never recover. Dropping it also
509		// releases its buffer.
510		for idx := range gs.data {
511			if idx >= m {
512				delete(gs.data, idx)
513			}
514		}
515	
516		missing := make([]int, 0)
517		for i := 0; i < m; i++ {
518			if _, ok := gs.data[i]; !ok {
519				missing = append(missing, i)
520			}
521		}
522		if len(missing) == 0 {
523			gs.markDone() // all data present; nothing to recover
524			return nil, nil
525		}
526	
527		if len(gs.data)+len(gs.parity) < m {
528			return nil, nil // fewer than M shards survive; recovery impossible so far
529		}
530	
531		codec, err := d.codec(m)
532		if err != nil {
533			return nil, err
534		}
535	
536		shards := make([][]byte, m+d.cfg.ParityShards)
537		for idx, payload := range gs.data {
538			// encodeDataShard's precondition is shardLen >= lenPrefixLen+len(payload).
539			// A wire-derived data payload that overruns the group's shard length would
540			// otherwise be silently truncated while its length prefix records the full
541			// length, feeding RS inconsistent bytes and fabricating recovered payloads.
542			// Fail fast instead of trusting the geometry.
543			if lenPrefixLen+len(payload) > gs.shardLen {
544				return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: data shard %d payload (%d bytes) does not fit group %d shard length %d", idx, len(payload), g, gs.shardLen)
545			}
546			shards[idx] = encodeDataShard(payload, gs.shardLen)
547		}
548		for j, pb := range gs.parity {
549			if j < 0 || j >= d.cfg.ParityShards {
550				return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: parity index %d out of range for group %d (K=%d)", j, g, d.cfg.ParityShards)
551			}
552			shards[m+j] = pb
553		}
554	
555		if err := codec.Reconstruct(shards); err != nil {
556			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: reed-solomon reconstruct group %d (m=%d k=%d): %w", g, m, d.cfg.ParityShards, err)
557		}
558	
559		sort.Ints(missing)
560		recovered := make([]Recovered, 0, len(missing))
561		for _, idx := range missing {
562			payload, err := decodeDataShard(shards[idx])
563			if err != nil {
564				return nil, fmt.Errorf("fec: decode reconstructed shard %d of group %d: %w", idx, g, err)
565			}
566			recovered = append(recovered, Recovered{Group: g, Index: idx, Payload: payload})
567		}
568		d.recovered += uint64(len(recovered))
569		gs.markDone()
570		return recovered, nil
571	}

574	func (d *Decoder) codec(m int) (reedsolomon.Encoder, error) {

A load-bearing architectural finding: **PROBE frames (`frame.KindProbe`) never traverse the scheduler/pacer** — they egress via `emitProbes()` (`internal/bind/probe.go:46-57`) writing directly to the socket, bypassing `Send`→`scheduler.Pick`→token-bucket. So "protecting probes from pacer shedding" (Q51) must be realized as shaping *DATA* egress to reserve link headroom, not as a `ClassControl`-style exemption. I also confirmed there is no scheduler→metrics seam today (`metrics.Source` = Paths/FEC/Reseq/Session/PeerNames only), that `updateGateLocked`/`setActiveLocked` are the transition-logging sites, that `SizePacingFromBDP` + `deriveWeightedPacingFromBDP` (G2/Q20) already exist and must NOT be re-owned, and that the e2e harness builds+runs the binary and scrapes `/metrics` via `metrics.Fetch`.

### Session summary
- **Role:** candidate planner (opus), goal G13 "Operability & safety for weighted aggregation + pacing", CANDIDATE mode (read-only; nothing written to any ledger).
- **Did:** Grounded read-only against the ledger (G13 + answered Q51–Q55) and the real repo via codegraph (internal/sched/weighted.go, internal/sched/scheduler.go, internal/config, internal/metrics, internal/device/metrics.go, internal/bind probe.go/multipath.go, internal/frame, internal/telemetry, test/e2e), then emitted one candidate task-DAG.
- **Achieved:** A 5-milestone / 6-task candidate honoring all binding scope decisions — item 1 observability (metric+log, per-peer, Q54), item 2 capacity-sanity guard (fail-fast declared / WARN+gauge undeclared, Q52), item 3(ii) PROBE-frame protection (Q51), the pacing-tradeoff + inner-ICMP-infeasibility docs, and an up-front shared e2e overload fixture (Q55) — while explicitly NOT re-owning G2/Q20's auto-derive/BDP docs (Q53). Counts: 5 milestones, 6 tasks.
- **Discovered (load-bearing):** PROBE frames never traverse the scheduler/pacer — they egress directly via `emitProbes()`→`WriteToUDPAddrPort` (internal/bind/probe.go:46-57), bypassing `Send`→`Pick`→token-bucket. So Q51's "protect probes from pacer shedding" cannot be a `ClassControl`-style exemption; it must shape DATA egress to reserve link headroom (I encoded this as a reproduce-first probe-egress reserve). Also: there is no scheduler→metrics seam today (`metrics.Source` = Paths/FEC/Reseq/Session/PeerNames), so item 1 needs a new snapshot accessor + `Source.Aggregation()` + collector gauges plumbed through Bind PeerSnapshots and the device metricsSource; `deriveWeightedPacingFromBDP`/`SizePacingFromBDP` already exist (G2 scope).
- **Issues:** The docs task (T4) cannot be e2e-gated (no runtime surface) — I flagged this explicit deviation from Q55's e2e-only rule and gate it on `just lint` + reviewer prose-check instead; behavioral tasks still update their own operator docs in-change per AGENTS.md.
